hub_subtitle: Inglês para Trabalho Remoto em Tecnologia & Quality Assurance

lesson.menu_desc: Walking a new teammate through a test case step by step -- precondition, input, trigger, outcome, and one edge case -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- Explaining a Test Scenario
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Claudia Veraldi | Lesson 7 -- Explaining a Test Scenario
lesson.grammar_point: sequencers and time linkers (first, then, after that, finally)

listenings.text: Welcome to the team. Let me explain how we write a good test case here. A test case is simple when you keep it in order. First, always check the precondition. Ask yourself: what must be ready before I start? For a login test, the account must already exist. Then write the steps in sequence, one step at a time. Describe the input clearly, say what the user clicks, and say what should trigger the check. After that, write the expected outcome, so anyone can see if the test passed. Finally, do not forget the edge cases. Try a wrong password or an empty field, because real users make mistakes. If you follow this sequence, your test case is clear, and any teammate can walk through it without your help.

Hi, it is Sofia. Here is a quick walk-through of the checkout test, step by step, so you can follow it. First, I set up the test account and open the checkout page. Then I enter the input: the product, the address, and the card number. Next, I click the pay button to trigger the payment check. After that, I wait for the outcome: a confirmation message appears on the screen. Before I finish, I also try one edge case, a card with no money, to see the error message. Finally, I save the results and submit the report. The whole sequence took ten minutes, and it was straightforward. Let me know if you want me to walk you through it live.

extra_audio.text: Here is how Ana walks a new teammate through the test case. First, she sets up the test account and opens the login page. Then she enters the input: the email and the password. Next, she clicks the button to trigger the login check. After that, she waits for the outcome: the dashboard opens. Finally, she saves the results and submits the report.
